name: Azure AI Foundry
description: Microsoft's enterprise model platform (Azure OpenAI + model catalog)

provider.comment: Operated by Microsoft as an Azure service. The platform was branded Azure AI Studio, then Azure AI Foundry, and is now being unified under the Microsoft Foundry name (checked 2026-07).

category.comment: Hyperscaler platform unifying agents, models, and tools under one Azure resource with Entra RBAC, networking, and policies; serves 1,900+ models from Microsoft, OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, and others behind one API.

api-launched.comment: Underlying Azure OpenAI Service inference API reached GA in January 2023 (widely reported via the Azure blog; that specific GA-date page was not re-fetched this pass). The Azure AI Studio/AI Foundry brand consolidated it later (Foundry evolution documented on the cited page); rebranded to Microsoft Foundry by 2026.

status.comment: Developer API is generally available; the core platform, models, and tool framework are GA, while some individual newer tools/models remain in preview (checked 2026-07).

api-docs.comment: Official Microsoft Learn documentation for Azure AI Foundry / Microsoft Foundry.

website.comment: The Foundry portal (ai.azure.com) is the product home; the marketing page is azure.microsoft.com/products/ai-foundry.

auth-methods.comment: Microsoft Entra ID identity with role-based access control (cloud-iam), managed identity (service-account), and resource API keys are all supported for authenticating requests.

self-serve-signup.comment: Self-serve via an Azure account and the Foundry portal — no sales contact required. Does require creating/having an Azure subscription (no standalone Foundry-only key issued outside Azure).

free-credits-trial.comment: New Azure accounts receive general promotional Azure credits (commonly USD 200 for 30 days) usable across Foundry; there is no dedicated standing free-inference tier for models (checked 2026-07).

regional-endpoints.comment: Available across many Azure regions and geographies (US, Europe, Asia Pacific); Global and DataZone deployment types control where processing occurs within a chosen geography.

data-residency-options.comment: Standard deployments process prompts/responses within the customer-specified geography; DataZone deployments keep processing within a data zone (e.g. US-only or EU-only) — a contractual data-residency guarantee, not merely multiple endpoints.

model-breadth.comment: Model families from many vendors: Microsoft, OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta (Llama), Mistral AI, Cohere, DeepSeek, xAI, NVIDIA, Stability AI, plus Hugging Face community models.

open-weight-models.comment: Open-weight models available including Meta Llama, Mistral, DeepSeek, and a large Hugging Face community selection (serverless or managed-compute deployment).

model-version-pinning.comment: Azure OpenAI models are pinned by immutable dated version ids (e.g. o1-2024-12-17, gpt-4.1-2025-04-14), so a specific snapshot can be deployed and called.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: Supports fine-tuning (custom models available exclusively to the customer) and Managed Compute deployment of your own/private model weights on dedicated VMs through the same platform.

model-catalog-size.comment: Documentation states more than 1,900 models in the Foundry Models catalog (the marketing page cites 11,000+ counting all Hugging Face/community models); checked 2026-07.

chat-messages-endpoint.comment: Modern multi-turn chat via the Chat Completions API and the newer Responses API (Agents v2) on the unified /openai/v1/ routes.

streaming.comment: Token streaming (SSE) is supported on the chat-completion/completion/responses/real-time operations of Azure OpenAI models (standard feature; checked 2026-07).

tool-function-calling.comment: Function calling is a documented built-in capability: the model requests a function, your app executes it, and the result flows back into the conversation.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: OpenAI models on Azure emit multiple tool calls in a single turn (parallel_tool_calls) for concurrent execution; availability depends on the underlying model.

structured-output.comment: Server-enforced Structured Outputs validate responses against a strict JSON Schema (response_format json_schema) on supported OpenAI models; looser JSON mode (json_object) is available more broadly. Many non-OpenAI catalog models support JSON mode but not strict schema (checked 2026-07).

system-prompt.comment: Distinct system/developer role supported in the messages array (system, developer, user, assistant).

image-input-vision.comment: Vision-capable models (e.g. GPT-4o, Llama vision) accept images in user messages as links or base64 data.

audio-input.comment: Audio input supported via GPT-4o audio / real-time operations (audio_tokens) and Whisper transcription models on the same platform (checked 2026-07).

document-pdf-input.comment: Documents/PDFs can be uploaded via the Files API / vector store and consumed through the File Search tool as first-class input rather than pasted text.

image-generation.comment: Image generation available through the same account (Image Generation built-in tool and image models such as DALL-E / GPT-image); checked 2026-07.

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: Speech/audio synthesis available via Azure OpenAI TTS models and GPT-4o audio output / real-time operations on the same platform (checked 2026-07).

embeddings-endpoint.comment: Text embeddings available via the embeddings operation (e.g. text-embedding-3) under the same API.

prompt-caching.comment: Automatic prompt caching (no cache breakpoints to set) for GPT-4o and newer; requires >=1,024 identical leading tokens, cache reads billed at a discount (up to 100% off input on Provisioned). Extended retention up to 24h on newer models.

batch-api.comment: Global Batch API processes asynchronous request files with a 24-hour target turnaround at 50% less cost than global standard, with separate enqueued-token quota.

max-context-window.comment: GPT-4.1 series on Foundry supports a 1,000,000-token input context window (per Azure's GPT-4.1 announcement; that specific page was not re-fetched this pass, so no source URL is cited). Real-world availability varies by region/SKU/API version. Checked 2026-07.

extended-reasoning.comment: Reasoning models (o-series, GPT-5 series) expose reasoning at the API level (reasoning_tokens; reasoning-effort control), documented in the dedicated reasoning-models guide.

built-in-web-search.comment: First-party Web search / Grounding with Bing Search built-in tool retrieves real-time public-web information with inline citations via the Agent Service.

built-in-code-execution.comment: Code Interpreter built-in tool runs Python in a sandboxed environment for data analysis, math, and chart generation.

mcp-support.comment: Shipped MCP tool connects agents to remote/local Model Context Protocol servers (key, Entra managed-identity, or OAuth auth); Foundry Toolboxes also expose bundles as MCP-compatible endpoints.

files-retrieval-api.comment: Files API / vector store plus the File Search and Azure AI Search built-in tools provide managed retrieval/RAG over uploaded documents the agent can reference.

agents-assistants-api.comment: Foundry Agent Service offers a higher-level stateful agents API (Responses API / Agents v2; Assistants API in preview) with managed conversation state, tool orchestration, and memory.

openai-compatible-api.comment: Exposes stable v1 OpenAI-shaped routes (/openai/v1/) usable directly with the OpenAI() client via a base-URL swap; unified project client also provided.

official-sdks.comment: First-party Azure SDKs (azure-ai-projects and Azure OpenAI clients) cover Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, .NET, Java, and Go; the OpenAI SDK also works against the compatible endpoint.

native-api-style.comment: Primary style is OpenAI-shaped (Chat Completions / Responses on /openai/v1/); non-OpenAI catalog models on Managed Compute use Azure cloud-native ML endpoints.

webhooks-callbacks.comment: Batch/long-running job completion is polled via status endpoints (validating/in_progress/completed states); no first-party HTTP webhook/callback for job completion was confirmed this pass (checked 2026-07).

published-sla.comment: Azure publishes an availability SLA (commonly 99.9%) for the service plus a 99% token-generation latency SLA on Provisioned deployments. Exact figures/tiers not re-verified against the specific SLA document this pass (checked 2026-07).

rate-limit-model.comment: Per-deployment TPM/RPM quotas (Standard) plus reserved capacity via Provisioned Throughput Units (PTU); Batch has a separate enqueued-token quota.

provider-fallback-routing.comment: Not a cross-provider routing gateway. A preview Model Router can pick among OpenAI models by prompt, but there is no automatic cross-vendor failover like a gateway (checked 2026-07).

status-page.comment: Covered by the public Azure status page (status.azure.com) and Azure Service Health; that page was not re-fetched this pass, so no source URL is cited (checked 2026-07).

no-training-default.comment: Microsoft states prompts, completions, embeddings, and training data are NOT used to train any foundation models without the customer's permission, and are not shared with OpenAI/other providers. Models are stateless during inference.

zero-data-retention.comment: Inference is stateless (no prompts/completions stored in the model). The default 30-day abuse-monitoring store with human review can be turned off entirely via approved Modified Abuse Monitoring for eligible/regulated customers.

compliance-certifications.comment: Inherits the Azure compliance program (the data-privacy page links to Azure compliance offerings): SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001/27018, HIPAA (BAA), GDPR, FedRAMP High, and PCI DSS among 90+ certifications. Scope can vary by feature (checked 2026-07).

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: Microsoft Entra identity provides SSO/SAML, unified role-based access control, network isolation, and Azure Policy governance.

pricing-model.comment: Primarily per-token pay-as-you-go (Standard/Global Standard); reserved capacity via Provisioned Throughput Units (PTU); some Managed Compute catalog models billed hourly by compute time.

unified-billing.comment: A single Azure subscription/invoice covers all models and vendors reachable through Foundry.

cache-batch-discount.comment: Batch is 50% less than global standard; prompt-cache reads are billed at a discount on input tokens (up to 100% off on Provisioned deployments).

cheapest-entry.comment: Lowest-friction paid start is self-serve pay-as-you-go per-token pricing via an Azure account; new accounts may also apply general promotional Azure credits.
